_publications._publicationTitle: SVA retrotransposon insertion-associated deletion represents a novel mutational mechanism underlying large genomic copy number changes with non-recurrent breakpoints.

_contextSnippet: In order to amplify across the inserted SVA element and the deletion breakpoints, we performed PCR using primers with a high melting-temperature and PCR conditions optimized to amplify GC-rich sequences
details: Successfully amplified across inserted SVA elements and deletion breakpoints using high melting-temperature primers and GC-rich sequence optimized conditions
